italy.name: Italy
italy.tagline: Home is where the heart is.

expeditions.name: Grimwood
expeditions.description: If you believe what the old people say around here, there once lived a very popular king in this wood. The wood`s original name has been long forgotten and is now only known by the name Grimwood. Rumour has it, that this king made a pact with the dark side so that he could continue to reign forever. However, Grimwood is an extremely sinister place, full of wild animals and impenetrable brushwood. Countless numbers of hunters and lumberjacks went too deep into the woods, never to be seen again. If you believe the gossip, the old king took them into his lair so that they could serve him for all eternity...
expeditions.additionalInfo: This is the first dungeon so Rat is really easy. Lynx could be easy but Lynx varies in levels too much - 2 to 5. The stats of a level 5 enemy could be too much for the beginner gladiator so do not fall into despair if you loose some of the battles against Lynx. Wolf is also strong so don't expect to start defeating him properly before level 5. The big question is whether you start trying the next expedition Pirate Harbour on level 5 or fight the Wolf. I would say stick with Wolf unless you are really twinked low level. The boss - Bear is really profitable if you could start beating it early (before level 8) because the Gold reward and experience are really high.

expeditions.name: Pirate Harbour
expeditions.description: On the coast in the south-east there is a pirate harbour. There are a lot of shady characters and goods that would be forbidden elsewhere there. Uninvited guests aren\`t welcome. Still, many adventurers and nosy people try their luck. Mostly they end up as food for the sharks. But if you bear up against all these pirates, you`ll get exotic treasures.
expeditions.additionalInfo: It becomes available too quickly. At level 5 you are most likely not able to kill Wolf in Grimwood even. If you are uber geared and twinked you may start with Fled Slave but that's not entirely easy. If you can fight without losing too many battles fight here. The experience and honour is not that much more than Grimwood but the gold is and gold is really important. Enemies gradually become harder with the boss - Captain raises to level 15-17 which in normal rates is almost impossible to beat at the low levels. Assassin is also really strong with huge variety in the levels. Gold boost is also big with Assassin and Captain.

expeditions.name: Misty Mountains
expeditions.description: The highest pinnacles of the Misty Mountains are hidden from the eyes of wanderers, because of the thick clouds surrounding them. Well, it`s not as if many people go around there anyway. It is a dangerous area, not only because of the dangers from the mountains themselves, but also because it is said that there are monsters in the caves in the southern slopes - the sort longing for human meat...
expeditions.additionalInfo: Same as Pirate Harbour, the entry level is a bit low for the creatures you have to fight in this expedition. The real level is more like 15. Elusive Recruit can be farmed easily but the rest are just plain hard. Harpy and Cerberus are jumping with 4-6 levels more which is insane. Same as Pirate Harbour, 3rd enemy and Boss are really a lot stronger than what you would be able to normally fight at this level.

expeditions.name: Wolf Cave
expeditions.description: In the hills south of the barbarian village there is a cave system that is called wolf cave by the townsfolk. As the name suggests, a large pack of wolves pack live there which often attack the surrounding farms during harsh winters. Some say this wolf pack is led by a large white wolf, but that may merely be the idle chitchat of simple farmers.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Typical expedition. Nothing unusual about it. Of course the low entry level doesn't mean that you can fight here at 15. Problem is that you will probably not fully discover the expedition as the fighting level is around 20 and at 20 you are able to travel to Africa. However, Africa is really hard at level 20 so you might want to stay here a bit longer and fight in Wolf Cave. What's interesting about the expedition is that the boss - Werewolf awards really good experience, this is if you are able to fight him without losing too much, otherwise he is not worth if you lose 50% of the fights.

expeditions.name: Ancient Temple
expeditions.description: It is long forgotten for which God this temple was erected for. Nonetheless, the old, simple ruin still has an aura of mysticism and power. This distant place of worship towers majestically above the lowlands - the wind blows eerily through the old ruins and many animals are nested there. Still - or maybe even because of all this - treasure hunters come here time and again. There is still the rumour that the true treasure of the temple has never been found...
expeditions.additionalInfo: The expedition is the first one you encounter when you return to Italy from your adventures abroad. A very convenient and interesting dungeon sits in this expedition. What's interesting is that Cultist Guard is really good in terms of rewards. 2nd, 3rd and Boss are not huge difference compared to Cultist Guard in terms of treasure. However, Cultist has a lot of Armour. Rewards from the boss are really good. If you can fight him, go for it.

expeditions.name: Barbarian Village
expeditions.description: In the far east there is a barbarian village where barbarians live who have not yet acknowledged the greatness of the Roman Empire. Because they mug travellers and merchants every now and then, you are likely to find many treasures. As long as the Imperial Legion doesn`t take any steps against the barbarians, going there is still a daring adventure.
expeditions.additionalInfo: What's typical for the barbarians is that they all have low armour. If you are doing fine in Ancient Temple you might not rush and start all over discovering and doing a new expedition. Rewards are not a lot greater than the Ancient Temple. What's fascinating is the damage of the Berserker. The boss is strong but good drops there.

expeditions.name: Bandit Camp
expeditions.description: Near the city, hidden in the southern foothills of the Misty Mountains, there is a bandit camp. Bandits, smugglers, thieves and other outlaws bustle around there. Although the imperial legion sent troops there countless times, the camp was reconstructed every time.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Now this is a proper upgrade of an expedition. Difference from Barbarian Village to Bandit Camp is greater than the difference between Ancient Temple and Barbarian Village. Fight here if you can. The expedition doesn't have a dungeon on its own but this is where the Advanced Italy dungeons start to be accessible so you might want to stay here and fight. Interesting is that Assassinator is really hard and almost as good as the boss - Bandit Chief.

africa.name: Africa
africa.tagline: Be one of the first gladiators and proclaim the glory of Rome.

expeditions.name: "Voodoo Temple"
expeditions.description: It is the generous gift made by the Empire for the Umpokta. After the foundation walls and the building material were delivered, the Umpokta banished all Roman architects and finished constructing the building themselves. Religious reasons were pushed forward. Certainly the temple probably holds many secrets and treasures, which are protected by force of arms.
expeditions.additionalInfo: The expedition is ready as soon as you are able to travel to Africa at the level of 20. However, enemies are level 30+ so fighting here on 20 is mission impossible. Perhaps if you are good geared you could pull it off on 25. The dungeon is as well too strong for a level 20 so in general don't bother coming at level 20 unless you know what you are doing. Interesting fact is that all of the enemies here are with very low HP and Constitution. Seth Priest is godly and practically unbeatable while you are on relevant level. Great rewards from him but Charisma is comparable to a level 50+ creature.

expeditions.name: "Bridge"
expeditions.description: In the face of the climate and the local geology, wooden bridge constructions are not practical. For this reason the Empire built this unique piece of Roman engineering here in Africa. The Umpokta consider it a passage, which connects the world of life and the world of the dead. Numerous misunderstandings arise, due to this. Gladiators that guard the bridge are seen as guardians, who watch over the access to the afterlife.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Funny how strong Man Eater is. You better stay with Tax Collector. Bone Shaman is really strong. Best if you farm Tax Collector until next expedition is available.

expeditions.name: "Blood Cave"
expeditions.description: There\`s a rumour amongst the superstitious natives that magical rituals are held here. The historiography of the empire writes of no such events taking place there. The fact is though, that the cave`s cooling shadows attracts deadly animals.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Blood Wolf is pretty beatable. However Giant Beetle is really hard with an armour of 5-6k your attacks would not pierce with a lot of damage. Fire Dancer is not as strong as other 3rd expedition enemies compared to other expeditions. Same could be said for the Fire Demon. By far the strongest in the expedition but not as strong as other bosses compared to their expeditions.

expeditions.name: "Lost Harbour"
expeditions.description: Once a thriving Viking outpost, it has in the meantime become a shadow of its former self. A textbook example of the empire and its allies. The strange power of voodoo forced the blonde giants to their knees. It apparently still holds findings from the houses and ships of the Vikings, that aren`t of any interest to the Umpokta.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Crocodile is pretty weak so it can be attacked easily at lower level. Undead Holder is not as strong as well because he has very low Dexterity which means that he won't be hitting you a lot. Giant Water Snake is strong but beatable as well. Mokele Mbembe is a special case. Can spawn as low as Level 48 and stats are good but not impossible. There is one problem however. Its non-penetrable armor makes him a though opponent. At 7-9.5k it consumes almost all the damage a level ~40 gladiator can do.

expeditions.name: "Umpokta Tribe"
expeditions.description: As far as the Empire is concerned, it is the high-point of civilisation amongst the savages of the savannah and deserts. Primitive house construction and religious superstition are very prevalent in everyday Umpokta life. With their masks and threatening songs they frighten the tenderly strung amongst our citizens.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Now there are strange things about this expedition. Tribal Warrior and Tribal Magician are pretty doable. However Spirit Warrior has the 3rd biggest Armor in the game. Seth High Priest is one good example of a high end boss. His stats and rewards and treasures are so high that in terms of gold, experience and honour he is among the 110+ bosses. There is something weird about the expedition in my encounters. I did not encounter any item drops while there.

expeditions.name: "Caravan"
expeditions.description: A small shadow over the relationship between the Umpokta tribe and the Empire is this hijacked caravan. There are still plenty of goods in the desert belonging to our citizens. The negotiations are being drawn out because of communication problems.
expeditions.additionalInfo: They are not wearing a lot of armor as clothing and this is reflected in their Armour stat - it is low. Spy and Caravan Guard are pretty weak, while Elite Guard is strong but not unbeatable. Slave Merchant is generous and if you could manage to beat him without loosing too many battles, go for him.

expeditions.name: "Mesoai Oasis"
expeditions.description: Fruitfully it lies in the desert and fearful are its dangers. As one of the few oases here in this region it is a place of refuge for many animals looking to quench their thirst. The shady trees offer shelter from the sun and offer predators the possibility of getting some food.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Just like Caravan this is a pleasant and on the spot expedition. Not to mention that the animals are really good looking. Nothing out of the ordinary. Weak 1 and 2 and strong 3rd in the face of Demon Lion. Demon Elephant is a jewel in terms of treasures. It can reach item level of 108 which is on the maximum frontier of your gear at this point and it will be a lot of purples. He can drop Ichorus so he might surprise you with a new weapon, especially if it rolls **Purple** or **Orange** quality.

expeditions.name: "Cliff Jumper"
expeditions.description: In the religious life of the Umpokta a surreal ritual has become tradition. On every third new moon the villages` priests meet and choose someone especially gifted. This person is accompanied by a procession to Cliffjumper Mountain. On arrival the chosen ones throw themselves jubilantly off the cliff to their deaths, it is said that it is a gift to the spirits. Aside from the spirits the numerous wild animals that have settled in this area also get some satisfaction from it.
expeditions.additionalInfo: This is the third of the pleasant high level African expeditions. Same as Mesoai-Oasis it is very standard. Weak Cursed Antelope and Giant Spider and very strong Shaman and High Shaman. High Shaman is considered one of the high end bosses in the game and is the highest creature in Africa. It's another question whether you will be able to effectively farm him until you leave this place on level 102 and to depart for Germania for good. Cursed Antelope has unnaturally low drop rate for items and Shaman seems to be drop really high item level, just like High Shaman. In your early 90s, try to farm Shaman or go back to Mesoai and try farming Demon Elephant. Mid 90's around level 95 you might be able to farm High Shaman more effectively. Do not farm him unless you win 9 out of 10 battles. It's just not worth it experience wise.

germania.name: Germania
germania.tagline: Prove yourself against the mighty Germanic tribes.

expeditions.name: Cave Temple
expeditions.description: We are born from ashes and ashes we will become. Doesn\`t it then make sense for us to devote a temple to the element that determines the cycle of life? If only there wasn`t that niggling fear. That it will eats its way into the souls of those who are supposed to be looking after the temple. And now the imposing, multi-story building stands stands deserted. Rumour has it that the fear living within the ruin of the building, has taken on a life of its own. It has supposedly got so strong that the dead buried there have risen from their graves. None of the priests that have tried to investigate this phenomenon have returned from temple. Perhaps you will have more luck or or ability?
expeditions.additionalInfo: This is the first expedition once you arrive in Germania for the first time. It is a normal expedition with the exception of Soulless. This boss is just on another level. It is completely unbeatable on the current level with 8-9k Armour and 170 damage.

expeditions.name: The green forest
expeditions.description: Whoever likes green and aromatic things will find that the green forest is the perfect place for them. The odour of Taraxacum officinale, Viola riviniana or Oxalis acetosella bewitch the senses and give all the horrors, that emanate from the forest, some comfort. Wolves and bears suddenly start walking up-right and killing wanderers. The same goes for the usually friendly wolf pack. Whoever ventures into the forest at night can usually find magic ingredients, but the risk of dying is just a bit too much for the average citizen. But who ever even listens to the opinion of an average citizen?
expeditions.additionalInfo: Boar is really weak so it's doable as soon as you hit 45. You could try Swamp Lord because he is not very strong. You could even try Swamp Spirit. The creatures in this expedition are quite weak with the exception of the Werebear who, same as Soulless, is practically unbeatable on 45-50 levels. The Damage of the Werebear is Divine. It is 333 which is the damage of a 100 level creature.

expeditions.name: Cursed Village
expeditions.description: The fertile flora and fauna of the area attracts many tribes. That is why it is only a question of time before the Vandals will arrive as well. They are dreadful companions yet they brew heavenly beer and like to tell stories of their battles. While intoxicated they are very unpredictable and have a penchant for attacking strangers. Pray for a good moment should you have to stay with them for awhile.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Hun is really weak so you can start farming him as soon as you hit 50. Ancient is stronger than a usual 2nd. Has a lot of Damage and Armor. Nachzehrer is the same. Not great stats for a 3rd but the Armor and the Damage are really nasty and probably won't let you farm him. Abomination is not that impressive, he is just really though. High Agility and Armor makes his skin impenetrable.

expeditions.name: Death Hill
expeditions.description: The loss of the cave temple poses a considerable problem to the dwellers: Where to put the dead? Death Hill had for a long time been a perfect answer. Until the dead started rising and feeding off the carrion. Naturally this attracted allot of wild animals. Still many adventurers don`t let this story deter them from looting the riches found in the graves.
expeditions.additionalInfo: What is special with this expedition are two things. First, all creatures are really weak. You can probably start farming Lich if you are a stronger than usual 55 Level. Necromancer Prince of course is a boss and won't expect to farm him but he is doable. Quite weaker than the rest of the bosses from previous expeditions here in Germania. The second special thing about Death Hill is that its dungeon is not for 55 Level. It's for 80 so you won't be changing the Viking Camp dungeon when you move on to this expedition.

expeditions.name: Vandal Village
expeditions.description: The fertile flora and fauna of the area attracts many tribes. That is why it is only a question of time before the Vandals will arrive as well. They are dreadful companions yet they brew heavenly beer and like to tell stories of their battles. While intoxicated they are very unpredictable and have a penchant for attacking strangers. Pray for a good moment should you have to stay with them for awhile.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Vandal Village is the first high level expedition which you will encounter on your 2nd and last return to Germania after Africa and Cliff Jumper. The expedition should pose a lot of difficulties as the journeys in high level Africa must have been fruitful and you should be coming back to Germania good geared. Farming Africa's Caravan, Mesoai-Oasis and Cliff Jumper would make any gladiator ready for high level Germania and Vandal Village. Time to play with the big boys and the phat loot. Vandal Warrior is very weak, as well as Jarl. Jarl might give you some problems at first but you will be farming him anytime soon if not immediately. Dark Fighter unfortunately is another story. High stat, insanely high Armor he might be too big a bite. Death Knight is even worse. He is a proper high level expedition boss so you have to pretty wild in order to farm him. What you need to concentrate while in Vandal Village is how to level up quickly to 100 and move on to Mine.

expeditions.name: Mine
expeditions.description: The pride of this region is its pure ore, which is stored in the mine. But for a while now a wolf pack has been living inside the mine. Nobody knows why they are there!..The workers have already themselves for the new dangerous work situation. Every loss is painful, but the region cannot live without ore. Everyone who will help them will be celebrated like a Caesar!
expeditions.additionalInfo: Mine is the first expedition that can let you drop and farm Orange items, although with latest patches it is possible for an Orange to drop earlier, here i where it all starts to get serious. Leveling after 100 becomes slow so you have time to get strong and start farming the expedition boss. Something that by now you probably didn't manage to do effectively. Well, now you will start doing it. Get strong and gear up to the point that you would comfortably farm Tatzelwurm. Before you can farm Tatze, go for all 3 opponents. Get all 4 extra bonuses by farming him so you can be prepared to farm Tatze on the maximum rewards. Do not think to rush anywhere. Teuton Camp will become available while you farm Tatze but Teuton Camp will not be as farmable as Mine so you would stay in mine a lot longer than you think. Months if not years. In terms of stats, Stone Golem and Tatze are actually quite good. It's just that the gladiators at this level start to level up very slowly and have time to gear up properly.

expeditions.name: Teuton Camp
expeditions.description: The Romans fear the aggressive and wild Teutons who live in this camp. They kill anyone and everyone who approaches. Apparently they even skin animals and humans! Beyond that they use the skin and fur to dress themselves. A respected citizen of Rome can hardly even imagine such a thing. The forest looks kindly upon the barbarians, even if it stinks like mead and decay around them. Only the bravest, or should we say those undaunted by death, go anywhere near to those savages.
expeditions.additionalInfo: We can say a lot about Teuton Camp but the reality is that it is a lot harder than Mine and it becomes available a bit too quick. On 104 you have just managed to start farming Tatze comfortably which might cause a problem coming here. Seidr is extremely hard. Very decent geared gladiators at level 107+ would still have problems with him. He hits like a truck and is also really high in Agility which makes it hard to hit him back. This essentially makes him a lot harder than Tatze and this discourages gladiators of ever farming Teuton Camp properly. At this level you would only aim to farm the expedition boss so if you can't do it properly here, better not do it at all and stay back in Mine for as long as you have to and then swtich to Koman Mountain directly. Nevertheless, you should still strive to obtain all the bonuses for Seidr and get him ready as soon as possible if the opportunity to switch from Tatze to Seidr introduces itself.

expeditions.name: Koman Mountain
expeditions.description: Majestically it stands there, the sacred Koman Mountain. Many mountaineers wish for nothing more than to climb this mountain. Unfortunately a lot of blood-thirsty animals living here have been responsible for converting the brave mountaineers into dead mountaineers. But of course something like that could never happen to a mighty Gladiator.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Majestic creatures and majestic loot. This expedition houses some of the highest level creatures in the game. It is also home to the magnificent Dragon boss who has a main role in every gladiator's high level adventures. Infernal Springbok and Sabre-Tooth Tiger are weak. Dragon Whelp is very strong. A senior adventurer would find problems defeating the Dragon Whelp on occasions. Because Teuton Camp might be inconvenient for some gladiators, a lot of adventurers will find themselves in Koman Mountain coming straight from Mine and Tatzelwurm. For those who do that, coming here will be a brand new experience. The loot is so much better. By now you would have worn out Tatze's clothing which predominantly is in the 110-115 item level range. Dragon will drop items in their 120s item levels, award 20+ experience on kills (with bonuses) and gold in 15000s. A real beauty. The only problem is that Dragon is hard. The good news is that not much harder than Seidr. Actually sometimes it would feel that Seidr might be even stronger than Dragon. If you are trying Koman Mountain and the Dragon farm, do not allow more than 1 loss per 10 battles. Otherwise it would be better to continue safe with Tatze until you are truly ready for Koman Mountain. Perhaps a pact for Charisma and Agility and a good Orange/Red forged weapon will help you start farming Dragon comfortably even at 108 Level. Getting all bonuses, same as with Tatze and Seidr is absolutely mandatory.

expeditions.name: Dragon Remains
expeditions.description: Anyone can feel his skin crawl when they reach this place. Magic passes through the scattered dragon bones. But this amount of free magic can not go unnoticed and the creatures who hunger for this force are drawn to this place. They claim that space and have set watchmen to guard it. But with great power comes great madness and famine that now threatens to engulf everything.
expeditions.additionalInfo: Dragon Remains was introduced into the game with the sole intention to solve the problem of high level gladiators having no challenges. In terms of expedition we can hardly say that Dragon Remains is anything unusual. The stats gradually raise over Koman Mountain and so is the loot. But that's only gradually. The real interesting part are the two new dungeons! As soon as you hit level 110, put on your Eagly Eyes costume and come here and start building your path for later levels. Because of Dis Pater costumes a lot of the time you won't be able to properly fight here before level 112 but it will be enough to get you ready for Dracolich. Leveling at 110 is so slow that by the time you hit 112 and be able to normally come to this expedition you should prepare to give it a try against Dracolich but be prepared - he is a formidable foe and is harder than Dragon from Koman Mountain. As for the creatures in the expedition. Lemures has 2-3k Armour which is puny. Same goes for Ritualist. He might be weaker than his Koman Mountain counterpart - Dragon Whelp.

britannia.name: Britannia
britannia.tagline: Subdue the British barbarians and prepare the land for the arrival of the Roman Empire.

expeditions.name: "Bank of the Thames"
expeditions.description: Galerius Senecianus\` Diary: DCXCIX a.u.c. We`ve finally found a suitable landing point! The journey through the night was difficult and I have not experienced such an uncomfortable climate in all my travels in the empire. Whilst sailing we have already been greeted with spears flung from the coastline. The local tribes, as it appears, will not be giving up without any resistance. Hopefully our large war barges will provide us with some covering fire as we land...

expeditions.name: "Forest Fortress"
expeditions.description: Galerius Senecianus` Diary: DCXCIX a.u.c. After the initial skirmishes, these barbaric Britons withdrew to the forest. Caesar pursued them with the intent of running them down and setting up an inland camp. To be honest, I am a little concerned. I heard things on the route here of mystical beings that are roaming and rampaging in the local woods. I pray that these are merely tales to frighten the young... and us Romans.

expeditions.name: "The Moor"
expeditions.description: Galerius Senecianus` Diary: DCXCIX a.u.c. We were able to subdue the domestic tribes with ease. As an offering of peace, each of them presented us with hostages, who then revealed the leader of the resistance. He goes by the name of Cassivellaunus and he has an encampment to the north-east of us inland. We immediately headed out and even made good progress. That was, until the sole route led us into and through this most eerie moor...

expeditions.name: "Camp Cassivellaunus"
expeditions.description: Galerius Senecianus\` Diary: DCXCIX a.u.c. We managed to put the moor behind us. My shoes and those of my companions are soaked to the core from sinking in the quagmire. Our commander Gaius Trebonius... not so much. To be quite honest, there is something about him that seems off to me. Caesar gave him the order to press forward into Cassivellaunus` camp, which is protected by both a wall and a ditch. Cassivellaunus also realised his inferiority in open battle and has released the majority of his armies. All that remain are his agile chariot riders and a few mercenaries who lie in wait and then ambush us...

expeditions.name: "Kent"
expeditions.description: Galerius Senecianus` Diary: DCXCIX a.u.c. The camp fell, Cassivellaunus fled! He however managed to send a message to the Kings of Kent, to mobilise them against us. They intend to attack our camp on the coast, hoping to make us retreat. They know the land and its intricacies much better than we do, but if we plan on staying here long term, we have to defeat them...

expeditions.name: "The Ford"
expeditions.description:
Flavius Eunapius` Diary: DCCXCVI a.u.c: Britannia... what did I do to deserve this. A long way from my wife, child and my beloved Rome. Slowly, memories of the stories my grandfather told me come to the fore of my mind: his grandfather assisted Julius Caesar in the subjugation of this barbaric people! And today? After the retreat and the initiation of kings approved by Rome, these barbarians returned to their uncivilised lives... until one of them came and implored help from Emperor Claudius.
The local troops were being led by two brothers, who had barricaded themselves up in northern Camulodunum. We were able to emerge victorious from the initial skirmishes after our landing. Now on our way north, we need to cross this river...

expeditions.name: Camulodunum
expeditions.description: Flavius Eunapius` Diary: DCCXCVI a.u.c: The battles were hard, the leader Caratacus was able to flee, but we triumphed! I have seldom seen such self-sacrificing opponents. One of them even stood to fall by the sides of his men in battle. It is a true leader that dies by the sides of the men he leads. Not like ours, Aulus Plautius. Because of his ineptitude, Emperor Claudius has deemed it necessary to travel to Britannia himself. He is bringing the urgently needed reinforcements as well as his infamous war elephants...

expeditions.name: "Cambria"
expeditions.description:
Flavius Eunapius` Diary: DCCCIV a.u.c: We have been in Britannia just about 10 years now, even though my wife first refused to come. In the meantime we have it pretty good here. We can call a nice estate with servants aplenty our own and for the most part, our lives are much better here than they were in Italy. Just... it is almost impossible to stop complaining about the weather...
Despite all this luxury, I have decided to head back into battle as an Officer. As Camulodunum fell after 16 days, the leader of the resistance was once again able to flee and since then has become somewhat of an icon in the resistance against us Romans. Now our spies have been able to locate him in Cambria, the kingdom of the Silures. I see it as my duty to stand against them in battle, as a duty to my fallen comrades, to finally bring this man to justice...

expeditions.name: "Mona Isle"
expeditions.description: Flavius Eunapius` Diary: DCCCX a.u.c: I feel my time drawing to a close. My children have followed my invitation and now accompany me through my final days. Looking back over my life, I made the right choice in lingering in Britannia. In my final battle I personally captured Caratacus and dispatched him to Rome, where Emperor Nero then princely rewarded us. My oldest son strove to follow my lead after some time and has already managed to attain a high standing within the military. Now he has the task of taking care of the final point of retreat for the Druids: Mona Isle. Even when I think highly of his ability, the power of the Druids makes me worry...
